topic: Someone finds out their medical records were mixed up with another patient's
raw_output:
[story 1]

The printout lay spread across the sterile white desk, a grotesque mosaic of unfamiliar diagnoses and procedures. Sarah traced a line with a trembling finger, the ink blurring under the pressure. Her breath hitched, shallow and quick, as she reread the date of birth—it wasn't hers. A cold knot tightened in her stomach, radiating outward. She looked up, her eyes wide, scanning the quiet office for an exit, a lifeline. Every sound in the room seemed amplified, sharp edges against her nerves. She pushed the papers away, the movement jerky, and suddenly felt a profound disconnect, as if she were watching a performance of her own life from a distance. The fluorescent lights hummed with an unbearable intensity.

[story 2]

Mark stared at the file, his jaw slack. The handwriting on the intake forms was elegant, familiar, yet utterly wrong. He blinked several times, trying to force the image into coherence, but the pieces refused to lock. A strange pressure built behind his eyes, making the room swim slightly. He leaned back in his chair, the cheap plastic groaning under his weight, and rubbed his temples hard. His hands started to shake, a fine tremor running up his arms. "This can't be right," he managed, his voice a thin, reedy sound. He picked up a pen, but the tip hovered inches above the paper, paralyzed by the sheer illogicality of the situation.

[story 3]

When the nurse slid the folder across the counter, David’s gaze snagged on the name scrawled at the top. A sudden, dizzying lurch pulled at his equilibrium. He felt a rush of heat flood his face, immediately followed by a chilling numbness creeping into his extremities. He tried to speak, to ask for clarification, but the words caught in his throat, forming only a dry rasp. He looked down at his hands, flexing his fingers slowly, as if checking to see if they were still attached to him. The room seemed to tilt, the corners of the walls seeming to shift into impossible angles.
